–(The highlands lying between the Euphrates River and the Mediterranean 
Sea)
–Called ARAM, from the son of ShemGe 10:22,23;Nu 23:7;1Ch 1:17;2:23
–During the time of Abraham it seems to have embraced the region 
between the Tigris River and the Euphrates RiverGe 24:10; with 25:20
–Including Padan-aramGe 25:20;28:5
–Minor kingdoms within the region
  .Aram-zobah, also called, ZOBAH and ZOBA1Sa 14:47;2Sa 8:3;10:6,8;1Ki 11:23;1Ch 18:5,9;19:6
  .And in the title ofPs 60
  .Geshur2Sa 15:8
  .Aram-rehob, also called BETH-REHOB2Sa 10:6,8
  .Damascus2Sa 8:5,6;1Ch 18:5,6
  .Hamath2Sa 8:9,10
–Conquest of
  .By David2Sa 8:3-13
  .By Jeroboam2Ki 14:25,28
  .By Tiglath-pileser, king of Assyria2Ki 16:7-9;18:33,34
–People of, colonized in Samaria by the king of Assyria2Ki 17:24
–Confederates with Nebuchadnezzar2Ki 24:2;Jer 39:5
–The Roman province of, included the land of CanaanLu 2:2,3
–And it included PhoeniciaMr 7:26;Ac 21:3
–The fame of Jesus extended overMt 4:24
